metadata.description: Agent-era engineering skills for Claude Code

plugins.name: groundwork
plugins.description: Requirements engineering before you build: dig out the real need behind a solution-shaped ask, map the silent stakeholders, write verifiable acceptance criteria, draw the scope line, and manage change — across a five-stage gated flow. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.

plugins.name: load-bearing
plugins.description: Architecture design for the agent-assisted era: concentrate scarce human judgment on the irreversible decisions (data schema, public contracts, trust model, the split into services) and let agents move fast behind the reversible ones — choosing style, stack, boundaries, contracts, and data model across a six-stage gated flow. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.

plugins.name: flightline
plugins.description: Set up or harden a project's engineering process so quality has an automated floor instead of depending on anyone's discipline — version control, code style, code review, CI/CD, and dependency/build management — across a six-stage gated flow, tuned for a world where agents write much of the code. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.

plugins.name: assay
plugins.description: Risk-driven test planning and hardening: choose what to test, the test type, the test doubles, and the cases that actually catch bugs, across an eight-stage gated flow. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.

plugins.name: gauge
plugins.description: Engineer a codebase's feedback surface so an agent gets clear, fast, local, attributed, hard-to-fake-green signal — strict typing, boundary validation, errors-as-values, structured failures, observability — across a five-stage gated flow. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.

plugins.name: stationkeeping
plugins.description: Take a system from merged to reliably serving users in production and keep it there: deployment & release strategy, infrastructure-as-code & config, observability, monitoring & alerting, SLOs & incident response, capacity, cost, and backup/DR — across a seven-stage gated flow, tuned for a world where agents operate production and read a green dashboard as safety. Note: the gates are enforced by the `checklist` CLI, which installs separately — see the repository README.
